dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (nih as attention-direct)
notes: Sentence-initial 'nih' directing attention to something the speaker is showing. 'Lihat ini' = 'look at this'. English 'Here, look at this' preserves both the offering-attention marker and the imperative. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: this is the most common nih use in casual conversation — does the comma after 'Here' land naturally or does 'Here look at this' read better?
